prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear grandchild, a CPU is like the brain inside your computer, you see—it does all the thinking and deciding what everything else should do. It's a little box of magic that tells the screen to show pictures, helps the keyboard type your words, and makes games work just like a conductor leading an orchestra. I don't quite understand all the fancy details myself, but I know it's what makes the whole machine come alive and do its job. Isn't that something special? And you, with your clever questions, are going to figure out even more about it than I ever could!
